item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I find this interesting but still lacking. Compulsory effects are always hard to work with, and sometimes end up being repetitive.
Nonetheless, this has some merit and a little extra effort could make into something very good.
Maybe an actual backstory for the object could help?

Also some tone issues that prevail in the researcher's dialogue and notes ruin the build up.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I feel like the voices that D-3704-7 heard, barring his father, were those killed in the fire.

I feel like this is overly speculative for a scientist, he has no real evidence for this idea. I understand that this suggestion should be made in the file, but perhaps this line could be revised to acknowledge that the idea isn't well-supported. Something like "Though I don't have hard evidence to support this, I can't help but feel like…"

Other than that, I think this was effective and well done. +1.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded: Alright, thank you for doing that, I wasn’t aware. Where is that stated? Maybe I skipped over it in one of the guides, or something.

item.title: Staff Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded: Author, I've cleared the tags as there are multiple tags missing that should be here. Unless you are very familiar with our tagging system, we generally ask people to wait for tagging staff to get around to it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
VERY well made, especially for a first SCP! +1 for sure!

Only one typo that I noticed, but it's minor: "When a subject comes within 2 meters of SCP-3704 and have been exposed longer than 5 minutes, a tertiary effect appears."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Geez, That went from 0 to crazy in no time.
Also, great first SCP.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This your first SCP? Damn…
Please take my +1.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Well, guys, this is my first SCP. Let's see how it goes!

I'd like to thank everyone that looked it over for me, including Jazstar, DrBlackBox, and Kunckles35. Seriously, this wouldn't be up on the site if not for you guys. Thank you.
